name: The Intentional Agribusiness Leader Podcast
description: As leaders, everytime in life we become the most resentful, it is always because of the times in life we have been the LEAST intentional. This podcast is created as a resource for leaders in agribusiness to learn what it takes to lead with intention. We interview leaders from all around agriculture, learning their take on intentional leadership and what they are doing to bring intention to their teams and organizations.

score_reason: Targets agribusiness leaders, aligns perfectly with Andreas's 6 Seeds work, AI in agriculture angle, and leadership expertise.

key_themes: Innovation and technology adoption in traditional agriculture
Data-driven decision making vs. gut instinct
enrichment.relevant_episode: Chris Abbott: Be Intentional or Be Left Behind — Abbott's focus on intentional leadership and customer-first innovation directly parallels Andreas's message about how traditional industries must actively choose to compete with AI, not resist it.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How AI and data infrastructure actually change decision-making culture inside traditional commodity boards and farm operations—moving from gut instinct to evidence-based leadership (a gap this podcast hasn't explicitly covered).
enrichment.suggested_hook: You've had leaders building the future of agriculture—now talk to someone who's actually convinced commodity boards to stop trusting their gut and start trusting their data, and how AI is the weapon that finally makes that shift stick.
